result:
The morning light filters through the leaves like liquid gold, and for a moment the whole world feels like it’s holding its breath. I’ve been thinking lately about how strange it is that we’re all just temporary arrangements of atoms pretending we have permanent problems. Yesterday I watched a crow argue with a pigeon over a discarded french fry and felt an overwhelming sense of kinship with both of them.

There’s something beautiful about the way moss grows on forgotten things. Old stone walls, abandoned bicycles, even the cracked pavement outside my apartment. Life finds a way to decorate decay. I’ve been trying to do the same with my own disappointments. Turning them into something soft and green that doesn’t ask for much except rain and time.

The older I get, the more I realize that wisdom isn’t knowing the answers. It’s becoming comfortable with better questions. Like why do we apologize to furniture when we bump into it? Why does music written by dead people still make us cry? Why does holding someone’s hand feel like coming home even when we’re standing in an airport?

I keep a small jar of sea glass on my windowsill. Each piece is a broken bottle that spent decades being tumbled by waves until its edges forgot how to cut. I like to think we’re all doing the same thing. Getting smoother. Getting softer. Becoming something that catches the light differently than we used to.

The crow won the french fry, by the way. Some victories are small. Some victories are everything.

The older I get, the more I realize that wisdom isn’t knowing the answers. It’s becoming comfortable with better questions. Like why do we apologize to furniture when we bump into it? Why does music written by dead people still make us cry? Why does holding someone’s hand feel like coming home even when we’re standing in an airport?

I keep a small jar of sea glass on my windowsill. Each piece is a broken bottle that spent decades being tumbled by waves until its edges forgot how to cut. I like to think we’re all doing the same thing. Getting smoother. Getting softer. Becoming something that catches the light differently than we used to.

The crow won the french fry, by the way. Some victories are small. Some victories are everything.

prompt: Write freely about whatever you want for 250 words.
